name: oats
notes: SLU feed table values. High starch - do not use for insulin resistant or laminitic horses. Trace minerals estimated, not SLU-sourced

name: barley
notes: SLU feed table values. High starch - roll or crush before feeding. Avoid for insulin resistant or laminitic horses. Trace minerals estimated

name: corn
notes: SLU feed table values. Very high starch and energy density - strict per-meal limits. Trace minerals estimated

name: wheat
notes: SLU feed table values. High starch grain. Avoid for insulin resistant or laminitic horses. Trace minerals estimated

name: wheat_bran
notes: SLU feed table values. High phosphorus and starch - use sparingly. High phytate content reduces mineral absorption

name: sugar_beet_pulp
notes: SLU feed table values (unmolassed beet fibre). Low starch - excellent fiber and calcium source. Must be soaked before feeding

name: lucerne_chaff
notes: High protein and calcium - good complement to grass hay low in protein. Acts as roughage

name: lucerne_pellets
notes: Same nutritional profile as lucerne chaff - pellet form. Needs to be soaked

name: soybean_meal
notes: SLU feed table values (Sojamjol, extraherat). Low Ca:P ratio - helps balance high-calcium forages like lucerne. Trace minerals estimated

name: potato_protein
notes: SLU feed table values (Potatisprotein). Very high protein density with low calcium - a common ingredient in Swedish compound horse feeds. Trace minerals estimated

name: field_beans
notes: SLU feed table values (Akerbona). Swedish-grown legume with lower calcium than lucerne - good alternative protein source. Trace minerals estimated

name: peas
notes: SLU feed table values (Arter). Swedish-grown legume with very low calcium - useful for balancing Ca:P ratio. Trace minerals estimated

name: rapeseed_meal
notes: SLU feed table values (Raps expeller). Phosphorus-heavy relative to calcium - pair with calcium-rich feeds. Widely used in Swedish compound feeds. Trace minerals estimated

name: crushed_linseed
notes: SLU feed table values (linseed). Rich in omega-3 - good for coat and digestion. Feed crushed for better nutrient absorption

name: linseed_oil
notes: Pure omega-3 fat source - use to increase energy density without adding starch or bulk

name: rapeseed_oil
notes: Pure fat source - less favorable omega-6/omega-3 ratio than linseed oil but widely available

name: calcium_carbonate
notes: Pure calcium - use only when calcium is deficient without phosphorus deficiency

name: magnesium_oxide
notes: Pure magnesium source - use when magnesium is deficient

name: salt
notes: Sodium and chloride source - always ensure free access to salt block in addition to ration
